beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Standard morning routine; low energy from yesterday (45/100) means a quiet start. Drizzle outside makes staying in longer appealing.

beats.activity: Walk to office in light rain
beats.reasoning: Georgetown to Georgetown is a short walk; rain is steady but light, so walking is still reasonable rather than driving. Keeps the commute minimal.

beats.activity: Standup, check Slack, start design sprint work
beats.reasoning: Typical Tuesday morning work block. Rain outside makes indoor focus easier; no urgent deadline but steady progress on design reviews.

beats.activity: Grab lunch supplies from nearby deli, head home
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon makes any cross-lake commute gridlocked; Kwame stays local. Rain is still steady, so a quick errand before heading home to work remote for the afternoon.
Short walk home; rain is still falling, but Kwame prefers indoor work during downpour anyway.

beats.activity: Work remote from home: design mockups, email, Figma iterations
beats.reasoning: Rain continues until 2pm; working from home is practical and aligns with Kwame's mood (low energy). Spreads out lunch, takes breaks to check vinyl prices online.

beats.activity: Lunch at home: sandwich and coffee
beats.reasoning: Rain is ending; Kwame eats light and stays in. Updates expense spreadsheet while eating—part of his meticulous routine.

beats.activity: Continue design work, finish afternoon standup notes
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Kwame stays home to finish the sprint work before the day winds down. Avoiding peak traffic around SR-520 closure.

beats.activity: Walk to record store to browse vinyl; Browse vinyl, chat with staff, find a reissue
beats.reasoning: Weather has cleared; Kwame's spontaneous side kicks in. A short walk to his favorite neighborhood record spot to hunt for deals and escape the work mindset.
Short walk back; evening is setting in. Kwame heads home to settle in for the evening.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and eat; spin new vinyl, play chess online
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home; Kwame cooks a simple dinner, listens to his new find, and plays a few chess games to wind down. Low energy day calls for introspection.

beats.activity: Relax at home: more vinyl, spreadsheet updates, light reading
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep; Kwame is already at home. Continues his meticulous expense tracking and enjoys quiet solo time, reflecting his introverted preference after a low-energy day.

diary:
Woke to that familiar Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you grateful for a short commute. The walk to the office felt meditative, rain pattering on my jacket. Standup went smooth, but honestly, the SR-520 chaos everyone was texting about made me feel lucky working from Georgetown. Around mid-morning I ducked to the deli, grabbed supplies, and decided to work from home for the afternoon. Something about the grey weather just called for it.

Design sprints hummed along. By early afternoon, restlessness crept in—the kind that needs breaking. I walked over to Sonic Boom around 2:30, and that was exactly right. Found a reissue I'd been hunting for months. The staff guy and I geeked out for a bit. Spun it over dinner, played some chess online, felt the day settle into something quiet and good.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure shaped my whole approach today—I saw coworkers stressed about traffic and made a quick call to stay home once the morning standup wrapped. Felt like the right move, especially since I can design just as well from my desk. The deli run was practical, but I think I also needed to move around, shake off screen time. By afternoon, that restlessness was real; I wasn't just procrastinating, I genuinely needed the record store. It's my pressure valve. Finding that reissue felt like the day rewarding me for staying flexible. The evening was exactly what I needed: vinyl, chess, spreadsheet tinkering—nothing demanding, just enough structure to feel productive without burning out.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
